statement.markdown: Given a Trie data structure, insert multiple words into the Trie using the `insert` function. However, the function currently fails to handle a specific edge case where a word's prefix is already present in the Trie but the word itself is not.
statement.inputFormat: A list of words to be inserted into the Trie.
statement.outputFormat: Whether the insertion was successful for each word.
statement.notes: The Trie is initialized with an empty root node.

examples.explanation: The word "app" shares a prefix with existing word "apple" but is not inserted.
The word "at" shares a prefix with existing word "cat" but is not inserted.

hints.text: Check for existing words in the Trie before inserting new words

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: Insert words into the Trie while checking for existing words.
editorial.bruteForce: Perform a depth-first search of the Trie to check for existing words.
editorial.betterSolution: Utilize a Trie's built-in properties to efficiently check for existing words.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a prefix search to check for existing words before inserting new words.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Verify the correctness of the optimal solution.
editorial.dryRun: Walk through an example of inserting words into the Trie.
commonMistakes: Failing to check for existing words

starterCode.java: public class Trie { public boolean insert(String word) { // TODO: implement } }
starterCode.python: class Trie: def insert(self, word: str) -> bool: pass
starterCode.javascript: function Trie() { this.insert = function(word) { // TODO: implement } }

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Trie { public: bool insert(string word) { if (root == nullptr) root = new TrieNode(); bool inserted = false; for (int i = 0; i < word.size(); i++) { inserted = true; root->children[word[i] - 'a'].insert(word.substr(i + 1)); } return true; } private: TrieNode* root; };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Trie { public boolean insert(String word) { if (root == null) root = new TrieNode(); boolean inserted = false; for (int i = 0; i < word.length(); i++) { inserted = true; root.children[word.charAt(i) - 'a'].insert(word.substring(i + 1)); } return true; } private TrieNode root; }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Trie: def insert(self, word: str) -> bool: if not self.root: self.root = TrieNode() inserted = False for i in range(len(word)): inserted = True self.root.children[word[i] - 'a'].insert(word[i + 1:]) return True
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function Trie() { this.insert = function(word) { if (!this.root) this.root = new TrieNode(); var inserted = false; for (var i = 0; i < word.length; i++) { inserted = true; this.root.children[word.charCodeAt(i) - 'a'].insert(word.slice(i + 1)); } return true; } }

solutions.python:
class Trie: def insert(self, word: str) -> bool: if not self.root: self.root = TrieNode() for ch in word: if ch not in self.root.children: self.root.children[ch] = TrieNode() self.root = self.root.children[ch] return True

def create_trie(lst): trie = Trie() for word in lst: trie.insert(word) return trie

# Test the function
trie = create_trie(['apple', 'app', 'orange'])
print(trie.insert('at'))  # Should print: False
solutions.cpp: class Trie { public: bool insert(string word) { if (!root) root = new TrieNode(); for (char ch : word) { if (root->children.find(ch) == root->children.end()) root->children[ch] = new TrieNode(); root = root->children[ch]; } return true; } private: TrieNode* root; };
